mentions.note: Earnings beat 39¢ ($2.18 vs. consensus); raised full-year guidance substantially. Five quarters of Aetna improvement; Medicare Advantage pricing mastered. Stock up 7.7% to 3-year high. Turnaround working — integrated health services model emerging.
Thinks CVS will give good quarter; CEO David Joyner doing great job fixing CVS while Rite Aid closed and Walgreens self-immolates; Aetna buried within CVS should be strong.

Owns 8,932 drugstores, UnitedHealth synergies strong. Trades at 11x earnings despite compression from Rite Aid bankruptcy and Walgreens pullback. CEO Joyner delivered great quarter; consolidation tailwind.
Caller bought after GE Healthcare disappointed; Cramer bullish: CEO David Joyner is real deal creating national drugstore chain; recommend hold/buy more.
Preferred healthcare/beauty retailer; higher quality than struggling cosmetics names.

Least real national drugstore chain now; less drugstore than managed care (Aetna); government Medicare Advantage reimbursement disaster; CEO David Joiner done terrific job; fascinating time to own; reports Tuesday.
Health insurance segment impaired by minimal Medicare Advantage rate increase; stock tumbled 14% despite owning UnitedHealth.
Better than UNH; higher yield; includes Aetna; David Joyner cleaned up problems; preferred healthcare play over UNH.
